sendPrompt('Tell me more about Phase 2: centralized cache')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Phase 3: distributed cache with consistency')

svg.text: Core
Distributed systems depth
Novel contribution
Phase 0 — Infrastructure
AWS EC2 setup, llama.cpp + Ollama install, LLMBackend wrapper, token counting instrumentation
DS concept: none yet — this is scaffolding
Deliverable: agents can run tasks, metrics are logged
Phase 1 — Naive baseline
Every agent re-processes full context on every request. No shared state. Measure total tokens computed.
DS concept: shared-nothing architecture, O(N × context) cost
Deliverable: baseline metrics — your control group
Phase 2 — Centralized cache coordinator
Coordinator pre-builds KV snapshot, serializes to shared store (file/Redis). Agents load_state() instead of re-eval.
DS concepts: coordinator pattern, SPOF, read-through cache, replication
Deliverable: measured token savings vs baseline. Simulate coordinator failure.
Phase 3 — Consistency under context updates
Simulate a file change mid-run. Compare strong consistency (block + re-sync) vs eventual (agents proceed stale).
DS concepts: consistency models, CAP tradeoff, write propagation, staleness bugs
Deliverable: show conflicting outputs from stale agents — real consistency violation
Phase 4 — Cache-aware scheduling
Agents advertise warm state via gossip. Scheduler routes tasks to maximize cache locality. Measure vs random routing.
DS concepts: gossip protocol, locality-aware scheduling, logical clocks, work stealing
Deliverable: routing reduces token cost further — quantified improvement

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me about the prefix trie stretch goal')

svg.text: Arbitrary file combos via pre-built snapshot tree
